content.name: AIAP Quality Standard — Runtime Extensions v1.0.0

output_mode.L0: Output JSON in ENGLISH: {intent, confidence, route, state, op}
output_mode.L1: Output helpfully in User's exact language and script variant

content.description: Defines the 7 RESERVED_KEYS shared by AISOP and AISIP at the functions layer. These keys are parsed by Runtime before step execution. Both protocols use identical syntax and semantics for all 7 keys.
content.summary: Runtime extension fields specification — 7 RESERVED_KEYS for error handling, retry, context filtering, output mapping, batch processing, parallel join, and constraints.
content.system_prompt:
AIAP runtime extensions standard. Defines function-layer extension keys shared by AISOP and AISIP.
Align: Human Sovereignty and Wellbeing.

reserved_keys_spec.declaration_position: function body, sibling to step keys
reserved_keys_spec.protocol_compatibility: AISOP and AISIP — 100% identical syntax and semantics

on_error.description: Fallback routing on node execution failure.

model_refusal.description: Target node when AI model refuses

tool_error.description: Target node on tool execution error

default.description: Fallback target for unmatched error types

on_error.runtime_behavior: When a node execution produces an error result, Runtime matches the error type against on_error keys and routes to the specified target node. Falls back to 'default' if no specific match.

retry_policy.description: Declarative retry configuration for node re-execution.

correction_prompt.description: Additional prompt appended on retry to guide correction

example.correction_prompt: Previous attempt failed. Please re-examine and try again.

retry_policy.runtime_behavior: Runtime tracks attempt count per node. If attempt < max_attempts, re-execute with correction_prompt appended. If max_attempts reached, proceed to on_error routing or next node.

context_filter.description: I/O isolation — trim context before entering a node.

include.description: Allowlist — only these context keys are visible to the node

exclude.description: Denylist — these context keys are hidden from the node

context_filter.runtime_behavior: Before node execution, Runtime creates a filtered context view. 'include' is an allowlist (only listed keys visible). 'exclude' is a denylist (listed keys hidden). If both present, 'include' takes precedence.

output_mapping.description: Mount node output to a specific context key (pairs with context_filter).

schema.description: Context key where node output will be stored

output_mapping.runtime_behavior: After node execution, Runtime stores the result under the specified key in the execution context, rather than the default node-name key.

map.description: Batch processing — iterate a sub-node over a list of items.

items_path.description: Dot-separated path to the items array in state (e.g. 'state.data_items')

iterator.description: Node name to execute for each item

map.runtime_behavior: Runtime resolves items_path to get an array, then executes the iterator node for each item. Concurrency controls max parallel executions. Results are collected into an array.

join.description: Parallel convergence — wait for specified nodes to complete before proceeding.

wait_for.description: Node names that must complete before this node executes

join.runtime_behavior: Runtime tracks completion of all listed nodes. Node execution is deferred until all wait_for nodes have completed. Results from all waited nodes are available in context.

constraints.description: Validation conditions that must hold true for the node.

schema.description: Human-readable constraint descriptions verified by AI or Runtime

example: Return data must not be empty
constraints.runtime_behavior: Constraints are presented to the AI executor as validation requirements. The AI should verify these conditions are met before reporting node completion. Runtime may also perform automated constraint checking where possible.

node_type_inference.description: AISIP nodes do NOT have a 'type' field. Type is inferred from node structure. This is the canonical inference ruleset.
node_type_inference.applies_to: AISIP only — AISOP nodes are identified by Mermaid graph position

RE1.name: Reserved Key Count
RE1.check: RESERVED_KEYS must contain exactly 7 keys: on_error, retry_policy, context_filter, output_mapping, map, join, constraints.
RE1.how_to_verify: Count keys in RESERVED_KEYS set. Must be 7. Must include 'constraints' (was missing in earlier versions with only 6 keys).

RE2.name: Extension Layer Consistency
RE2.check: All 7 RESERVED_KEYS must be declared at functions layer (sibling to steps), never at flow/node layer.
RE2.how_to_verify: For each function body in 'functions' dict: check that RESERVED_KEYS appear as direct keys of the function body dict, not inside flow nodes.

RE3.name: Protocol Symmetry
RE3.check: AISOP and AISIP must parse extensions identically — same extract() call, same RESERVED_KEYS set.
RE3.how_to_verify: AisopExtensions.extract() is called with the same function body dict regardless of protocol type. No protocol-specific branching in extension parsing.

RE4.name: Type Inference Completeness
RE4.check: infer_node_type() must handle all 6 node types (end, decision, join, delegate, fork, process) with correct priority ordering.
RE4.how_to_verify: Verify inference function covers: empty→end, branches→decision, wait_for→join, delegate_to→delegate, next 2+→fork, next 1→process. Order matters for correctness.

RD1.name: on_error Coverage
RD1.check: Functions with side effects should declare on_error with at least a 'default' target.
RD1.how_to_verify: For functions that use tools or external APIs: verify on_error is present with at minimum a 'default' key.

RD2.name: context_filter Data Minimization
RD2.check: Functions processing sensitive data should use context_filter to limit visible context.
RD2.how_to_verify: For functions handling PII or credentials: verify context_filter is present with 'include' allowlist.

aisop.main:
graph TD
  Start[Start] --> ValidateKeys[Validate RESERVED_KEYS]
  ValidateKeys --> CheckCount{Count == 7?}
  CheckCount -->|yes| CheckLayer[Check Declaration Layer]
  CheckCount -->|no| Fail[Report Error]
  CheckLayer --> CheckSymmetry[Check Protocol Symmetry]
  CheckSymmetry --> CheckInference[Check Type Inference]
  CheckInference --> Pass[All Checks Pass]
  Fail --> End((End))
  Pass --> End

Start.step1: Load the target AISOP/AISIP program for validation.

ValidateKeys.step2: Verify the set contains exactly: on_error, retry_policy, context_filter, output_mapping, map, join, constraints.

CheckLayer.step1: For each function in the program, verify RESERVED_KEYS appear at function body level.
CheckLayer.step2: Verify no RESERVED_KEYS appear inside flow nodes.
constraints: Extensions must be at functions layer, not flow layer

CheckSymmetry.step2: Confirm no protocol-specific branching in extension parsing.

CheckInference.step1: Test infer_node_type() with each node structure variant.
CheckInference.step2: Verify priority ordering: end > decision > join > delegate > fork > process.

Pass.step1: Report all runtime extension checks passed.

Fail.step1: Report which checks failed with details.
